feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Landscape Transformation , Enterprise edition replacement Private Cloud
entry.content:
Hi

What is the Private Cloud equivalent for the On-premise 7018629 SAP Landscape Transformation , Enterprise edition to enable the Profit centre Re-org tool in ECC? as per on-premise note:(see SAP notes 1810605 (1810605 - General information about profit center reorganization - SAP for Me) and 1534197 (1534197 - SAP LT: Transformation Solution - Profit Center Reorganization - SAP for Me)

entry.title: MB51 Report Fields in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

The MB51 report in the public cloud is missing important fields like Receiving Storage Location, G/L Account, Receiving Batch, and Receiving Material etc. These fields are essential for the client's requirements.

Is there a way to get to these fields from the MB51 report on the public cloud?

thanks / Suresh.E

entry.title: Invoice issue for 3rd party without shipping notification
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team,

We are facing a major issue as part of Sales Processing using Third-Party without Shipping Notification (BDK) where system is copying the item quantity from Vendor invoice without any subsequent checks which is resulting in Invoices generated for a different quantity from Sales Order.

For example, we have a sales order where item 10 has quantity as 50. Order is saved, a purchase requisition was automatically created. Then the Purchase order generated with reference to requisition. 

The issue: Accidentally, we processed the Vendor invoice incorrectly with quantity as 50,000 instead of 50 by mistake. As soon as this was created, the Billing date on Sales order was updated and an Order related billing document was generated with 50,000 quantities. Now a customer invoice was incorrectly processed for 50,000 quantities, and the amount was automatically calculated as per the pricing condition records maintained in the system, rather than the price populated on the Vendor invoice.

Question: When creating the customer invoice in the Create Billing Documents app, shouldn't be there a safety checks or tolerance limit in these cases? Now SAP is automatically populating the quantity from Vendor invoice without any checks.

Please can you investigate the reason this has happened?

Thanks and regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Sales Commissions - External Sales Representative (2TT)
entry.content:
Good Afternoon, 

Can we define a new condition contract type for an External commission agent?

Thank You

SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

entry.title: CDS VIEW Project Responsible Person name on SAP Cloud
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 
we are trying to insert Project Responsible name on CDS to view all project managers in the report, but the element we add on CDS doesn't actually work. we joint it with the WBSElementBasicData and we opened the element as semantic element with "text", but still doesn't work. 


Does anyone know how to make it work ?

entry.title: Exchange Rate Conversion for Company Code Currency
entry.content:
In standard S/4HANA systems, M is the standard translation currency type for company code currency translation, and it cannot be altered for any specific company codes:

There is a legal requirement for specific countries to use rates provided by the national banks. It means, instead of usual Reuters rates (scope item 1S4) updating the exchange rate type “M”, a new source for foreign currency exchange rates will be needed in our current project for those countries.

We have followed this SAP documentation: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/currency-conversion-with-prescribed-exchange-rates/ba-p/12845598.

We proceed with all customizing suggested; however, this S/4 HANA Public Cloud implementation is a worldwide project and for countries that use the same currency (example: EUR, GBP) and requiring different sources for exchange rate declarations, Alternative Exchange Rate solution would not work.

We will have many rollouts to different countries, using the same currency (Example: GBP, EUR, USD), in case more than 2 countries use alternative exchange rate types, it would work only for one country. In this case, alternative exchange rate is a huge problem to be used because multiple countries use the same currency as their company code currency but use different sources for exchange rate conversions alternative exchange rate usage will only work for 1 of them, not for all.   

Example: Serbia, Turkey, Bulgaria have a legal requirement to use their own national banks as an exchange rates provider, and Reuters is setup for type M as a default provider for the countries already active in the system. By adding a new country we will be obliged to exclude this countries' ratios from Reuters. At some point, this will make a mess and will be hard to monitor.

Please provide us any tips, tricks, advices.

entry.title: No Shipping Tab in newly created document type for Returns by Copying NB / NB2
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I have successfully configured all the necessary prerequisites to enable the shipping tab for Returns Purchase Orders (PO) within our SAP system. These configurations have proven effective for the predefined SAP document types NB and NB2.

1. Assigned shipping points.
2. Configured delivery type and availability check procedures by plant.
3. Defined shipping data for plants.
4. Ensured that the customer role in business partners (BP) is maintained, with the "Return Supplier" field appropriately checked.

However, our requirements necessitate the creation of a customized document type for Return POs. To accommodate this, I copied the NB document type and created a new one. Following this, I configured the "Configure delivery type and availability check procedures by Plant," as it is related to the document type.

Regrettably, despite these efforts, the shipping tab does not appear for the newly created document type. A similar issue arose when attempting the same process with a document type created by copying NB2.


Q1. Are there any additional configurations required to enable the shipping tab in Returns POs? I note that in Private cloud/On-Premise environments, there exists a configuration activity as "Returns to Supplier," where delivery types for Returns are specified. However, I could not locate a similar configuration activity in the Public cloud environment.

Q2. Is it advisable to solely utilize the NB or NB2 document types for the Return to Supplier process? Or is it possible and recommended to create a new document type specifically for Return POs?

Thanks,

Sabarinathan K

entry.title: How to enable activation email in IAS for manual import from S/4HANA Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hello IAM/IAS Gurus,

How can I enable the activation email to be sent to each newly created user (via a manual import) in IAS Tenant?  

I use the import job to create users in bulk in IAS.  The import file is created from the backend S/4HANA public cloud tenant.

While the users are getting created in the IAS tenant, no activation email is sent to them.

As a workaround, I am manually initiating an activation email in IAS.  This process is time-consuming and does not send the link for the backend S/4HANA public cloud tenant.

How can I ensure that when each time a mass user import is performed, the newly created users created will get an activation email, along with the url/link to the specific back-end S/4HANA public cloud tenant from which the import file was created?  The underlined requirement is key, as the import file could be created from multiple S/4HANA public tenants (eg: Starter, DEV or TEST).

Thank you in advance and best regards,

Shaji

entry.title: SAP SD - Organizational Structure
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Requirement is a ad service company who has different product types calles weedmaps, canveya and ads and each product types will have different other services associated with it and also it has different pricing procedures. But customer wants to have a single invoice to be billed out for all these different products types with different pricing. How can we approach this by sales org, distribution channel and division? Or should I use any other field other than the org elements. I need to take a decision on how many sales org, distr chnl and division this customer can have. 
Please give me some advise.

Thanks!

SAP S/4HANA SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Sales SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud SD (Sales and Distribution)

entry.title: Manage Number Range Intervals Required For Standard Objects.
entry.content:
Our Requirement – As 'SNRO' option is not available in the Public Cloud, How can we maintain number range by selecting Object Name.

Let me give you an example,

In the ECC S4 HANA we have option with T – Code SNRO

In the SAP Public cloud, we have option like 'Manage Number Range Intervals' but in this app, we did not find any standard Objects –

Kindly Suggest if any option are exist in the Public Cloud.

@DennisLeslie  @SAPSupport @ANKIT_CHAUHAN

entry.title: Is extensibility possible to upload more than 999 line items by using Upload Customer Open Items app
entry.content:
Business Background:-

Our client has embarked journey of business transformation into S/4 HANA Public Cloud system. So the client has preselected scope item – J59 (Accounts Receivables) to upload customer open items (AR items) by using standard App - Upload Customer Open Item

Here the business team would be planning to use this app to upload customer open items on monthly basis hence they are intended to upload more than 2000 entries in single file.

Project Phase:- Explore

Issue Description: -

While testing the app - Upload Customer Open Item in starter system, the app is giving error message  if the upload file contains more than 2000 records

As per SAP help document , it is mentioned that each time you can upload no more than 2,000 entries in a file. If we have more than 2,000 entries in a file, split them up and upload them separately. As the maximum number of entries in an invoice during posting is limited to 999, avoid entering more entries than that number.

In current SAP ECC system of client, there is custom transaction code created which splits the file logically if items are more than 999 items. Hence the business team does not require it splits the file manually. so the business team is looking forward for similar solution where the app can able to split the files if entries more than 2000 (999 items) as it will require hugh manual efforts from business side to split the records into multiple files.

Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: How to upload Customer Master data in S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Our client has embarked journey of business transformation into S/4 HANA Public Cloud system. Here the client has requested to develop bespoke solution to mass upload customer master data as they need to create new customers on monthly basis in Production environment. We are aware that customer master data (BP) by using standard Data migration cockpit should be recommended approach. But data templates of DMC might get changed for Business Partners for every Cloud release so the business has raised concerned about adjusting templates.

Hence we would like to know if there is any standard best practice available for mass upload of customers (other than DMC)

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Is extensibility possible to upload more than 999 line items by using Upload Customer Open Items app
entry.content:
Our client has embarked journey of business transformation into S/4 HANA Public Cloud system. So the client has preselected scope item – J59 (Accounts Receivables) to upload customer open items (AR items) by using standard App - Upload Customer Open Item

Here the business team would be planning to use this app to upload customer open items on monthly basis hence they are intended to upload more than 2000 entries in single file.

Project Phase:- Explore

Issue Description: -

While testing the app - Upload Customer Open Item in starter system, the app is giving error message  if the upload file contains more than 2000 records

As per SAP help document , it is mentioned that each time you can upload no more than 2,000 entries in a file. If we have more than 2,000 entries in a file, split them up and upload them separately. As the maximum number of entries in an invoice during posting is limited to 999, avoid entering more entries than that number.

In current SAP ECC system of client, there is custom transaction code created which splits the file logically if items are more than 999 items. Hence the business team does not require it splits the file manually. so the business team is looking forward for similar solution where the app can able to split the files if entries more than 2000 (999 items) as it will require hugh manual efforts from business side to split the records into multiple files.

Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Inter-company Process without Sales & Distribution Module
entry.content:
Hi SAP Gurus,

Product: SAP S/4HANA Public CLoud 2402 Version.

Business Requirement: They want to perform Inter-company process without Sales & Distribution Module. How we are going to achieve this. Can anyone help us how we are going to perform this in SAP S/4HANA Public cloud instance along with Inventory accounting details + Invoice accounting details.

Thank you in advance!

Regards,

Kundan

SAP Analytics Cloud @saph4publiccloud @Sap

entry.title: Define Table(CDS) with nested structure like JSON
entry.content:
Hi Everyone

I want to create a 'define table ' with CDS view just like the below nested json

Later I would like to use this in fiori app also hence please suggest how to do

entry.title: SAP Subscription Billing, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Clarification
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a customer with SAP Subscription Billing solution, with integration of 57Z. Has anyone used this solution of 57Z? 

Please correct me if I'm understanding correctly.

First, we have to create subscriptions, rate elements and products in SUB BILL system and then subscriptions are replicated as provider contracts, rate elements and products are replicated as products in SAP.

In SAP we initially thought it has to be Sales Order or Sales Contract, but I tried to create a sales order with subscription products manually created inside SAP with SBPD, SBRE material type it said there is no item category available for subscription products, could anybody clarify that possible to create a sales order with subscription products?

Then I searched SAP help portal that it has to be a solution order in SAP with service items, sales items and subscription items can be added and when released it will create subsequent documents automatically.

But I can't find one created for subscription products. Also, should we need to create any subscription products manually inside SAP or only via replication handled? If so to create manually, do we need valuation class? Also, couldn't find one for subscription products created.

Also, replications is not working properly maintained below settings in the integration settings for Subscription Billing system.

Please correct me on the inputs of above data in screenshots and what should be the values for source tenant for customer ID?

Please advise.

Thanks!

SAP S/4HANA SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud SAP Subscription Billing SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management, subscription order management SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Sales SD (Sales and Distribution)

entry.title: Create New Controlling Area in S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi There,

We need additional controlling areas and it needs to be mapped to existing company codes, how to create in public cloud?

Thank you,

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Different exchange rate in RFUMSV00
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

we have to set a different exchange rate for RFUMVSA00/VAT Report. Journal entries are translated with exchange rate "M". But for the VAT report, the exchange rate given from the Bundesfinanzministerium (https://www.bundesfinanzministerium.de/Web/DE/Themen/Steuern/Steuerarten/Umsatzsteuer/Umsatzsteuer_Umrechnungskurse/umsatzsteuer_umrechnungskurse.html) should be used.

Is this possible in a public cloud system? And if yes, what SSCUI´s do I have to customize to get the desired report?

Best Regards and Thank you for any hints,

Sabrina

entry.title: Roadmap viewer - Business Driven Configuration Questionnaires for Asset Management
entry.content:
Dear @SAPSupport,

I was reviewing the business driven configuration questionnaire (BDCQ) for asset management for SAP S/4H Public Cloud (3-system landscape). 
When reviewing this BDCQ, I noticed that all the scope items on the questionnaire are the old scope items, which are depcrecated for S/4Hana Public Cloud (as of 2302). When will a new and more complete questionnaire be made available on the roadmap viewer? 

These questionnaires should be an aid to consultants in the prepare and explore phase, but unfortunately it is unusable at this stage. 
thank you in advance for your assistance,
kind regards

entry.title: Target cost, planned cost and actual cost in a production order / process order
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I want to analyze three different costs in my production orders / process orders for a manufacturing company. 
The planned values, will be based on a production BOM. The actual values will be based on the actual values in the confirmations of the order. However, my financial controller want to be able to set target costs with a standard cost estimate based on a universal BOM and then compare the planned costs and actual costs with target costs. 

However, I can have no success in creating a target cost to my production orders. 

Where do I configure so I can get target costs based on a standard cost estimate?

We are currently using actual costing (33Q), event based production cost posting (3F0) and standard cost calculation (BEG).
